## 📋 FOR HUMANS (What Happened)

**Primary Topic:** Fixed table formatting inconsistencies in Page 47 and created properly formatted Page 59 using corrected patterns

**What We Built:**
- **Page 59 - Direct Comparison Approach Table** (`PREVIEW-Master.html` lines 6149-6476)
  - 8 columns: empty header, SUBJECT, COMP 1-6
  - 5 table sections: Direct Comparison, Sale Information, Income Information, Physical Information, Unit Amenities & Adjustments
  - Summary rows with white backgrounds and proper spacing
  - All field mappings using standard pattern: `<span class="field-mapped" title="{{Field_ID}}" data-sample="value">{{Field_ID}}</span>`

**What We Discovered:**
- Page 47 had multiple formatting issues affecting data presentation
- Global CSS classes were being used inconsistently (monospace font on numeric cells, wrong alignment, inefficient column widths)
- Capitalization rate summary section needed visual grouping through spacing
- Table section headers should be centered, not left-aligned
- "Data Point" label in first column header was unnecessary and didn't match reference

**What Changed:**
- **Global CSS `.numeric-cell`** (line 105): Removed `font-family: monospace`, changed `text-align: right` → `text-align: left`
- **Global CSS `.label-col`** (line 103): Changed `width: 35%` → `width: 20%`
- **Global CSS `.table-section-header`** (line 123): Changed `text-align: left` → `text-align: center`
- **Page 47 City fields** (lines 3766-3770): Wrapped in `<strong>` tags for bold emphasis
- **Page 47 Capitalization Rate section** (line 3856): Added `border-top: 10px solid transparent` to AVERAGE row for visual grouping
- **Page 47 Table header** (line 3739): Changed `<th>Data Point</th>` → `<th></th>` (empty cell)
- **Page 59 complete implementation**: Added 328 lines of properly formatted comparison table

**Decisions Made:**
- **Decision:** Use left-alignment for all field values (not right-align for numbers)
  - **Why:** Consistency across all data types, cleaner appearance, matches reference image
  - **Impact:** All pages using `.numeric-cell` class now have left-aligned values

- **Decision:** Reduce label column width from 35% to 20%
  - **Why:** Longest label is "Capitalization Rate" (19 chars), 35% was wasting space and causing text wrapping in comparable columns
  - **Impact:** All comparison tables now have better space distribution (20% labels, 80% for comparables)

- **Decision:** Bold all city names in comparison tables
  - **Why:** Visual emphasis for geographic location, easier scanning
  - **Impact:** Page 47 and Page 59 have bold cities, should be applied to future comparison pages

- **Decision:** Group capitalization rate summaries with spacing
  - **Why:** Reference image showed visual grouping: (Capitalization Rate + HIGH) together, gap, then (AVERAGE + LOW) together
  - **Impact:** 10px transparent border-top on AVERAGE row creates clear visual separation

- **Decision:** Center-align table section headers
  - **Why:** Reference image showed "COMPARABLE SALES" centered, looks more professional
  - **Impact:** All blue table headers (`.table-section-header`) now centered

- **Decision:** Remove "Data Point" label from first column header
  - **Why:** Reference image had empty first header cell, cleaner appearance
  - **Impact:** Page 47 table header now matches reference format

## 📊 Technical Details

### CSS Changes Applied

**`.numeric-cell` (line 105):**
```css
/* BEFORE */
.numeric-cell { text-align: right; font-family: monospace; }

/* AFTER */
.numeric-cell { text-align: left; }
```

**`.label-col` (line 103):**
```css
/* BEFORE */
.label-col { background-color: #f0f0f0; font-weight: bold; width: 35%; color: #333; }

/* AFTER */
.label-col { background-color: #f0f0f0; font-weight: bold; width: 20%; color: #333; }
```

**`.table-section-header` (line 123):**
```css
/* BEFORE */
.table-section-header {
    background-color: #003B7E;
    color: white;
    font-weight: bold;
    font-size: 10pt;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    padding: 6px 8px;
    text-align: left;
}

/* AFTER */
.table-section-header {
    background-color: #003B7E;
    color: white;
    font-weight: bold;
    font-size: 10pt;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    padding: 6px 8px;
    text-align: center;
}
```

### HTML Pattern for Bold Cities
```html
<td><strong><span class="field-mapped" title="{{COMP_1_City}}" data-sample="North Battleford">{{COMP_1_City}}</span></strong></td>
```

### Capitalization Rate Spacing Solution
```html
<!-- Capitalization Rate + HIGH (close together) -->
<tr>
    <td class="label-col"><strong>Capitalization Rate</strong></td>
    <!-- values -->
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: white;">
    <td class="label-col" style="background-color: white;"><strong>HIGH</strong></td>
    <!-- value -->
</tr>

<!-- GAP created by border-top -->
<tr style="background-color: white; border-top: 10px solid transparent;">
    <td class="label-col" style="background-color: white;"><strong>AVERAGE</strong></td>
    <!-- value -->
</tr>

<!-- AVERAGE + LOW (close together) -->
<tr style="background-color: white;">
    <td class="label-col" style="background-color: white;"><strong>LOW</strong></td>
    <!-- value -->
</tr>
```
